key_insight: A deformable Cosserat framework lets standard softening plasticity models capture strain localization without changing the constitutive update or consistent tangent.
engineering_relevance: It offers a mesh-robust, easy-to-implement way to predict shear bands and post-peak response in geotechnical and other localization-prone solids.
method: Uses a macro-continuum Cauchy plasticity model with a micro-continuum director field.
Keeps dissipation in the macro-scale plasticity while the Cosserat micro-scale adds only linear elastic energetic terms.
Introduces an internal length that controls localization pattern selection and interaction, not artificial diffusion.
evidence_signal: Benchmarked on shallow foundation problems with Tresca and Matsuoka-Nakai plasticity, showing mesh-convergent load-displacement curves, dissipated energy, and shear-band patterns even for unstable post-peak responses.
watch_item: The approach adds extra Cosserat DOFs and residual/tangent terms, so implementation still requires careful finite element integration and calibration of the internal length scale.
paper_title: Strain localization in softening plasticity without modifying standard constitutive models: a deformable Cosserat approach
